name: Cala Blanca and Playa de la Galera - circular from Calabardina through the Regional Park Cabo Cope and Puntas de Calnegre
name_original: Cala Blanca y Playa de la Galera — circular desde Calabardina por el Parque Regional Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre
region: Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre
region_intro: Touring cycling in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre offers routes through a protected regional park along the Mediterranean coast of Murcia, Spain. The area features diverse landscapes, including a 17-kilometer stretch of coastline with beaches, secluded coves, and towering cliffs. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from arid Mediterranean flora and scrubland to dry riverbeds and coastal mountains. The geological formations include limestones and tilites, providing a distinct backdrop for cycling. Best touring cycling routes in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre The most popular touring cycling route is Cala Blanca and Playa de la Galera - circular from Calabardina through the Regional Park Cabo Cope and Puntas de Calnegre, a 14.0 miles (22.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores coastal formations and Mediterranean vegetation within the regional park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Cabo Cope – Cope Tower loop from Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope, a difficult 25.6 miles (41.3 km) path. This route offers continuous sea views and passes historical landmarks like the Cope Tower. Local touring cyclists also love the Percheles Beach – Women's Bath Beach loop from Playa del Siscal, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Touring cycling in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre is defined by coastal paths, Mediterranean scrubland, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;14&lt;/strong&gt; touring cycling routes in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from easy coastal rides to more challenging paths through the regional park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: The climate in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre generally allows for year-round cycling. However, for the most comfortable experience, consider visiting outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring and autumn offer pleasant weather for exploring the coastal and mountain scenery.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are &lt;strong&gt;5 easy&lt;/strong&gt; touring cycling routes available. These are generally shorter with minimal elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Cala Blanca – Cope Tower loop from Cala Blanca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that allows you to enjoy the coastal views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: You can expect varied terrain, blending Mediterranean mountains with coastal elements. Routes feature everything from fine black sand beaches and rugged rocky shores to Mediterranean scrubland and ramblas (dry riverbeds). Some paths offer continuous sea views, while others venture inland through arid flora. The geological structures include limestones and tilites, providing a distinct backdrop.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You can encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;, a 16th-century defensive bastion offering panoramic views. Cyclists can also discover secluded coves and beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4858540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Women's Bath Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy stunning vistas from viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6331635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calabardina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016753483/cala-blanca-and-playa-de-la-galera-circular-from-calabardina-through-the-regional-park-cabo-cope-and-puntas-de-calnegre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Blanca and Playa de la Galera - circular from Calabardina through the Regional Park Cabo Cope and Puntas de Calnegre&lt;/a&gt;, which explores coastal formations and Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.4 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from more than 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the continuous sea views, the diverse landscapes blending mountains and coast, and the opportunity to explore historical landmarks like the Cope Tower. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre offers &lt;strong&gt;6 difficult&lt;/strong&gt; touring cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant climbs and longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower – View of Cabo Cope loop from Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 51.2 km path with over 800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Is it possible to cycle with dogs on the trails?
A: While the regional park is a natural area, specific rules regarding dogs on cycling trails can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas and near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on certain paths.

Q: Where can I find parking for my bike when visiting Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points to the regional park and near popular starting points for routes, such as around Calabardina or Playa del Siscal. Look for designated parking areas near beaches or trailheads. As it's a natural park, facilities might be more rustic in some areas.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: While the regional park itself is largely undeveloped, you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation in the nearby municipalities of Lorca and Águilas. These towns offer a range of options for cyclists looking to refuel or stay overnight, providing amenities close to the park's boundaries.

Q: Can I reach the cycling routes using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly into the heart of the regional park are limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to access the various starting points of the cycling routes. However, you may find bus services to the nearby towns of Águilas or Lorca, from where you would need to cycle or arrange further transport to the park's trailheads.
tips: The views from the road are very beautiful. On the horizon you can see Cape Cope surrounded by a sea of turquoise waters.
There are beautiful views of the Playa Blanca located in the municipality of Lorca that we can reach by the GR 92 on foot or by bicycle. It has a nearby parking.

Wild beach within the Cape Cope Regional Park and Calnegre Points. It can be reached on foot or by bike along the GR-92 path that you can connect from Calabardina or at any point on the coast of the Cope beaches.

waypoints.name: Playa Blanca (Calnegre and Cabo Cope Regional Park)
